comment.body:
this is very important.
this is the furu-thing that is devastating, and built this wrong culture. chasing the trone of "the best trader".
but the best trader is not who is top ticking everything, and not even the one who generates the most alpha.
but the one who can sustain a stable growth with the less risk possible.
this is what many don't understand.
they play the guessing race instead, and lose.

i rather proud of the growth i generated via rational decisions, thatn the ones that were on pure luck (like in the first times).
furus created this wrong culture. and this is what Wall Street likes the most. they can push the crowd here and there by that

comment.body:
As Dalio Dixit:
" Remember that the news, to be of value, should be viewed in the context of the conditions of the big forces that transpire together to determine the direction of big cycle changes, most importantly changes in the monetary order, the domestic political order, the world geopolitical order, climate changes, and technology changes (i.e., the Big 5).
    Remember that we are on the brink of big changes in the monetary, domestic political, and international orders and that everything depends on whether or not these things are handled in smart, cooperative ways.
    Remember that, in your investing, it is important to 1) have a well-thought-out investment game plan that includes smart diversification that you're sticking with and to 2) not respond after the fact to news and market movements without careful consideration."

comment.body:
you know, this is also a crucial thing.

ppl usually have a mindset for bullish or bearish bias that is hardly changeable. This is natural.
to switch fast, is very hard.
to be versatile is very hard. Two different approach. (i mean, in trading, not in a relationship.. but there too)

so it is ok, if somebody understood the bear side, or the bullside of the tape.
it is okay if somebody starts to play only for short or long, if he has a tactics.
martingale, etc. sized right... just like that IC whale does in some weeks. He has a bias, he trades it. But he is definitely prepared. and via martingale he will take back the prvious losses.
mean reversion trading is bit riskier, written in the educational post.

but the main mistake is not cutting the loss. not recognizing when to quit, and wait for a better entry. 
this is why strategy and read matters.
comment.raw_body:
you know, this is also a crucial thing.

ppl usually have a mindset for bullish or bearish bias that is hardly changeable. This is natural.
to switch fast, is very hard.
to be versatile is very hard. Two different approach. (i mean, in trading, not in a relationship.. but there too)

so it is ok, if somebody understood the bear side, or the bullside of the tape.
it is okay if somebody starts to play only for short or long, if he has a tactics.
martingale, etc. sized right... just like that IC whale does in some weeks. He has a bias, he trades it. But he is definitely prepared. and via martingale he will take back the prvious losses.
mean reversion trading is bit riskier, written in the educational post.

but the main mistake is not cutting the loss. not recognizing when to quit, and wait for a better entry. 
this is why strategy and read matters.

comment.body: Mental flexibility is a fundamental quality in trading, but unfortunately, as you said, it's common to lack it and fall into biases, both rational and irrational, most of the time without realizing it. It requires a great effort of self-knowledge and willpower to create the necessary mental flexibility. It usually comes after a great deal of pressure and some big mistakes that make you rethink your pre-established mental framework.
